places.title: Hanoi Jeep Tour: Food, Culture and Fun by Vietnam Army Jeep
places.description: Experience the sights, sounds, and smells of Hanoi in a unique way as you explore the city in a Soviet-era GAZ-69 jeep. See a mix of city highlights and hidden gems in the capital of Vietnam and learn about the country’s history from your guide. Choose from morning, afternoon, or evening tours and enjoy a delicious street food meal.

places.title: Hanoi Motorbike Tour: Food, Culture and Fun by Vintage Motorbike
places.description: Discover the cuisine of northern Vietnam on a food tour of Hanoi. Hop on the back of a vintage motorbike and zoom through the streets of the city in search of delicious dishes. Choose from a morning, afternoon, or evening departure and enjoy tastings of delectable street foods as well as a complimentary drink over the course of a few hours.

highlighted_review.text: We also passed many of Hanoi’s iconic landmarks like the Long Bien Bridge

places.title: Small Group Hanoi Jeep Tour All Inclusive
places.description: This tour combines sightseeing, cultural immersion, and food exploration, making it an unforgettable experience!

highlighted_review.text: Great way to.see main landmarks in Hanoi.

places.title: Full-Day Ninh Binh Highlights Tour from Hanoi
places.description: Skip the hassle of piecing together boats and bike rentals and savor Ninh Binh’s highlights on this guided small-group tour. Visit Hoa Lu, once Vietnam's capital, explore Mua Cave and mountain views, ride a boat through Tam Coc caves, and cycle to Bich Dong Pagoda. Enjoy an authentic Vietnamese lunch and hotel pickup and drop-off by limousine minivan.

places.title: Hanoi Opera House
places.description: Resembling the Opéra Palais Garnier, Hanoi Opera House, built in 10 years since 1911 by two French architects. The magnificent building became one of the top landmarks in Hanoi. The Opera House hosts cultural entertaining performances, bamboo cirque, contemporary dance, orchestra concerts and government events. Come to the front gate to check availability and ticket to a performance in this iconic building.

highlighted_review.text: The Hanoi Opera House is an old city landmark.

places.title: Hanoi City Highlights Walking Tour – Landmarks & History
places.description: Uncover Hanoi's Untold Stories and the Vietnam War! Discover History & Culture, having FUN fun in the heart of Hanoi. Delve deeply into about Vietnamese's lifestyle. You Will Love it ❤ ❤   Highlight : • Why early ? -> Beat the crowd + the heat. • Small Group of Up to 6️⃣ people • Focus on History & Culture & Fun • Gain deep insight into Vietnamese lifestyle

highlighted_review.text: Great overall tour of Hanoi to see the key landmarks!

places.title: Hanoi City Tour: Private Half-Day Customized with Train Street
places.description: This private half-day tour of Hanoi is unique for its blend of iconic landmarks, cultural immersion, and local experiences. You'll visit the serene Tran Quoc Pagoda, the historic Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, and the thrilling Train Street, where a train passes inches from homes. Explore traditional craft streets in the Old Quarter, where artisans continue age-old trades, and end at the vibrant Dong Xuan Market, offering a taste of local life. The tour’s personalized nature allows for a deeper, more intimate connection with Hanoi’s rich heritage in a short amount of time.

highlighted_review.text: If you want to have a great overview of Hanoi's landmarks and history this

places.title: Chua Tran Quoc
places.description: Chùa Trấn Quốc (or Tran Quoc Pagoda) is located beside the dazzling West Lake, on Thanh Nien Road, Hanoi. Particularly, it is seated on an island linked by a bridge to the causeway between the two most romantic lakes of Hanoi: West Lake and Truc Bach Lake.

places.title: Vespa City Tour &Train Street Female With Ao Dai Riders 4,5 Hours
places.description: Take in some of Hanoi’s historic highlights and spectacular sights from the back of a Vespa scooter on this half-day tour. Instead of watching the sights fly past from the back seat of a crowded tour bus, enjoy the open air and get uninterrupted photos at landmarks like the Long Bien Bridge and the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum. Experience different ways of living in Hanoi, from farm life on the Red River to the hustle and bustle of the French Quarter.

places.title: Long Bien Bridge
places.description: The Long Bien bridge across the Red River connects Hanoi with the main port of Haiphong. Designed by Gustave Eiffel, the bridge sustained significant damage during the Vietnam War. Only half of the bridge retains its original shape, but a rebuilding project supported by the French government is currently in progress to restore the bridge to its original appearance.

places.title: Hanoi Instagram Tour: The Most Scenic Spots
places.description: Give your social media a boost while in Hanoi on this half-day small-group tour to capture visually inspiring content for your feed. Travel by motorbike or car, and visit locations such as Hanoi Train Street, Phung Hung Mural Street, Long Bien Bridge, and more, with an English-speaking guide providing deeper insights. Hotel pickup and drop-off in the Old Quarter area are included for convenience.

places.title: Imperial Citadel of Thang Long
places.description: Welcome to the Imperial Citadel of Thang Long – Hanoi! Situated at the heart of Hanoi, the Central Sector of the Imperial Citadel of Thang Long is an outstanding place of interest not only for the capital city but also for the country as a whole. The site is one of the ten special national heritage sites proclaimed by the Prime Minister in 2009 and was inscribed on the World Heritage List by UNESCO’s World Heritage Committee in 2010. Its Outstanding Universal Values are reflected in its historical longevity, its continuous role as a seat of power, and its multiple cultural layers. The World Heritage Site comprises two sections: the archaeological site at 18 Hoang Dieu Street and the central axis of the Nguyen Dynasty’s Citadel of Hanoi, which together create an integrated heritage complex. This was the most important sector of Thang Long Citadel, the capital of Dai Viet under the Ly, Tran, and Le dynasties from the 11th to the 18th centuries. It was also the core of the earlier Dai

places.description: Vivago Travel offers exclusive tours for international visitors to explore the beauty and culture of Vietnam. Guests will visit iconic destinations such as Ha Long Bay, Hoi An, Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City... With immersive and culinary experiences, Vivago ensures a memorable, enriching, and seamless journey through Vietnam's diverse landscapes.

places.title: Ninh Binh Day Tour from Hanoi: Hoa Lu Trang An and Mua Caves
places.description: Escape the bustling streets of Hanoi for a scenic day trip to Ninh Binh, often called “Halong Bay on land.” Nestled in the northern region of Vietnam, Ninh Binh is a stunning landscape of limestone mountains, tranquil rivers, and rich historical sites.

places.title: Hanoi Jeep Tours Led By Women: City & Red River Half Day
places.description: Explore a different side of Hanoi away from the tourist hotspots on this small-group tour through the city’s neighborhoods and backstreets. But instead of a stuffy tour bus, you'll explore via Jeep. You'll see unique sights like the Huu Tiep Lake and the city’s infamous ‘Train Street’, whizz past landmarks like Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, and more. You'll also stop to taste iconic foods and drinks, adding quintessential flavor to your adventure.

places.title: Bicycle Tours Hanoi

Specially,We arrange some new biking tours on the countryside. It is said that travel by bike ( or Motorcycle ) is the best way to discover Vietnam , either for old or young people recently because of the good condition of all roads, even in the countryside with a reasonable costs and flexible schedule, travelers can enjoy biking trip any time and any where to get unforgettable experience for a peaceful and restful local life in your journey..With our long experience working in tourism, we are sure to server you on the best way.Your satisfaction is our reputation.

places.title: Private Hanoi City Half-Day Tour with Train Street Visiting
places.description: The best way to explore Hanoi is on foot, and a private guide helps you navigate the city like a local. Cover Hanoi's major highlights and learn about its culture from your guide. You'll get personalized attention and can ask your guide as many questions as you like. Check off attractions such as the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex, Tran Quoc Pagoda, and the famous train street.

places.title: Hanoi Vespa Tours: Hanoi Countryside Vintage Vespa Tours
places.description: Discover the breathtaking charm of Northern Vietnam's rural landscape as you effortlessly glide past vast rice terraces and lush gardens.  Delve into Vietnam's vibrant history with a trip to the Co Loa Ancient Citadel Temple, where ancient tales and legends animate the ancient ruins.  Immerse yourself in the lively atmosphere of local markets, where the pulse of daily life resonates through the bustling streets.  Connect with countryside dwellers, swapping stories over a comforting cup of tea in their homes, and gaining insights into their distinctive way of life.  Wrap up your journey with a culinary treat, savoring authentic local dishes and indulging in Vietnamese egg coffee or delightful Mango Pudding dessert at a cherished local spot.

places.description: Discover the rich history and charm of Hanoi on a half-day food tour by scooter. Travel around the Old Quarter, Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and West Lake at a relaxed pace and see the city from a unique perspective. You’ll visit the most popular sights, where you can stop for plenty of photo ops.

places.title: Ho Chi Minh Museum
places.description: Wellcome to Ho Chi Minh Museum! President Ho Chi Minh - Vietnamese hero of national liberation and great man of culture. After he passed away on September 2nd 1969, in compliance with the Vietnamese people’s aspiration, the Communist Party of Vietnam’s Politburo decided to build the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and the Ho Chi Minh Museum. After 20 years of preparing and building, the Ho Chi Minh Museum was inaugurated on September 2nd 1990, on the occasion of the 100th birthday of the President. Ho Chi Minh Museum has more than 170,000 decuments, objects and films about President Ho Chi Minh’s life and revolutionary work. They are invaluable assets of the Vietnamese people in educating patriotism, studying and following Ho Chi Minh’s thought and moral quallties; and encouraging the Vietnamese generations to succeed failthfully and excellently his great revolutionary work.

places.title: St. Joseph's Cathedral
places.description: Entering this majestic church is like walking into 1886, as little has changed since it first opened its doors.

places.description: Embark on a thrilling half-day private scooter food tour in Hanoi and explore the city’s vibrant flavors and authentic cuisine. Experience the ride of a lifetime as you discover Hanoi’s unique culinary treasures, from the famous Obama bun cha to Aunt Tu’s renowned snail seafood stall. Satisfy your sweet tooth with a refreshing dessert experience, indulging in the delightful Mango Jelly made from luscious ripe mangoes. Conclude your motorbike tour with a visit to the enchanting Train Street Cafe. Book your spot on our “Half Day Private Hanoi Street Food Tour by Scooter” today and experience Hanoi like never before!

places.title: Thap Rua Tower
Hanoi Flag Tower
places.description: This place was built between 1805 and 1812 under King Gia Long and is over 2 centuries old. It resembles an old stone fortress and has steps leading to the top where one can get a good view of the city. There are old cannon sitting at the base of the place at grand level.

places.title: ViettrendTour

Unlike traditional travel agencies, we focus on creating personalized journeys that immerse travelers in the authentic beauty, culture, and history of Vietnam. With a strong commitment to sustainability and responsible tourism, we design unique itineraries that connect travelers with the heart of Vietnam, offering unforgettable experiences that go beyond the typical tourist route.
